mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals sustained household consumption expenditure, which drives corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when RSAFS is rising month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4.5% raises the cost of capital relative to historical norms, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) concurrent with a month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) jointly signal a contraction in both labor demand and manufacturing output, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month declines in PAYEMS and INDPRO do not produce negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of co-occurring instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when industrial production (INDPRO) declines over three months, a concurrent rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) accelerates the discounting of an earnings recession, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO is declining over 3 months, rising UNRATE coincides with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal accelerating residential investment and construction activity, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month HOUST changes coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month increase in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) erodes real corporate margins and reduces the present value of future earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL over a 3-month window does not coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point or greater rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over a three-month window signals labor market slack that triggers downward earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances of UNRATE rising 0.3pp or more in 3 months coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) confirm a broad-based expansion in supply and demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in INDPRO and RSAFS coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances.
